title: Registry Table Stress
subtitle: Dense semantic register with long definitions, status signals, owners, risks, and governance notes

rows.definition: Aligned view of systems, interfaces, ownership boundaries, reusable knowledge modules, and the operating scope they belong to.
rows.risk: Scope must match the declared domain boundary; hidden cross-domain ownership must be called out.

rows.definition: Authoritative document, PLM record, MOC package, or system record used to validate derived knowledge assets.
rows.risk: Revision mismatch invalidates derived content and must trigger re-review before reuse.

rows.definition: Condition that limits how an asset, parameter, process step, or reusable module can be reused across products.
rows.risk: Exceptions require explicit owner approval and traceability to the affected knowledge object.

rows.definition: Approved package that can be referenced by maps, taxonomies, procedures, dashboards, or workflow diagrams without rewriting the source logic.
rows.risk: Must cite source boundary, steward, and allowed-use conditions before downstream reuse.

rows.definition: Boundary where systems, teams, or process owners exchange data, decisions, events, or responsibilities.
rows.risk: Ambiguous ownership causes handoff gaps, duplicate records, and stale downstream knowledge.

rows.definition: Named category in a hierarchy used to classify knowledge objects while preserving parent-child meaning.
rows.risk: Do not skip parent levels unless a rule explains why the shortcut is allowed.

rows.definition: Measure used to evaluate completeness, freshness, approval status, or reuse readiness of a knowledge asset.
rows.risk: Metric definition must be stable enough for trend comparison across review cycles.

rows.definition: Pointer to an out-of-bound system or provider whose data influences the register but is not governed inside this knowledge boundary.
rows.risk: Provider schema or SLA changes may break assumptions without changing internal records.

rows.definition: Reusable response pattern for known deviations, incomplete evidence, late approvals, or temporary source gaps.
rows.risk: Playbook cannot become a permanent bypass for missing controlled-source evidence.

items.label: Yellow
items.value: governance, quality controls, active risk constraints

items.label: Green
items.value: approved reusable modules and reusable assets

items.label: Purple
items.value: external systems, optional links, out-of-bound references

info_panels.title: Registry Rules
items: Every term must resolve to one accountable owner and one evidence requirement.
Lifecycle state must describe current use permission, not project mood.
Long definitions should wrap inside cells without shrinking the register into unreadable type.

items.label: Cadence
items.value: monthly semantic review; immediate review on controlled-source change

items.label: Consumers
items.value: architecture, quality, document control, knowledge operations

annotations.text: Stress template: long rows, semantic badges, and governance panels should remain readable without turning the table into a dashboard.
